entries.title: Migrate from Camera2 to CameraX
entries.summary: Camera2 offers granular control but introduces boilerplate: managing `CameraDevice` states, `CameraCaptureSession` lifecycles, background threads, `HandlerThread`, and manual orientation calculations.

entries.title: Camera support for foldable devices
entries.summary: Foldable devices introduce unique challenges for camera applications, including dynamic layout changes, multiple display orientations, and physical device postures, such as tabletop and book modes.

entries.title: Low-light development guide
entries.summary: This guide covers implementing low-light features using **Night mode extensions** and **Low Light Boost (LLB)**.

entries.title: ML Kit spatial and coordinate mapping
entries.summary: When you use ML Kit for features such as face mesh, object detection, or pose detection, the most common failure point is the coordinate disparity between the analysis image and the viewfinder UI.

entries.title: testing.md
entries.summary: Automated testing for camera features is notoriously difficult because you can't easily mock physical hardware, lighting, or motion. This guide provides patterns for reliable, hermetic camera tests.

entries.title: Thermal-aware camera development
entries.summary: Camera operations are among the most power-intensive tasks on mobile devices. Without proactive management, the system throttle hardware, drop frames, or force-close the camera app.

entries.title: Wear OS camera remote development
entries.summary: Developing camera features for Wear OS is rarely about the watch's own lens, if it even has one. It's almost always about creating a **Remote Viewfinder** to control the phone's camera.

entries.title: Extended reality camera development
entries.summary: Developing camera features for XR devices, headsets, and AR glasses requires a shift from 2D pixel-pushing to 3D spatial awareness.

entries.title: CameraX skill

entries.title: Avoid unoptimized downloads

entries.summary: Apps Experience Program (AEP) is a program to recognize and reward great app experiences with a new program rate card to drive business growth for your app. Participation in this program is voluntary and apps that choose not to participate will not have a change in benefits they are entitled through…

entries.title: Get support
entries.summary: If you need assistance or have questions regarding the Apps Experience Program (AEP) guidelines, application review status, or service fee configurations, you can reach out to the Google Play support team.

entries.title: AEP guidelines overview
entries.summary: This document acts as a centralized resource for exploring and understanding the technical requirements of the Apps Experience Program (AEP).

entries.title: AEP guideline: Android MCP
entries.summary: > [!NOTE] > **Note:** ***Delayed enforcement notice*** . *Enforcement is deferred **until the > latter of March 1, 2027 or three months after the launch of self-service > registration** for Android MCP. Current AEP participants will receive advance > notice prior to the start of enforcement*.

entries.title: AEP guideline: CameraX
entries.summary: Integrate the [Jetpack CameraX library](https://developer.android.com/jetpack/androidx/releases/camera) to ensure high-quality, consistent camera capture across the diverse Android-powered device ecosystem. Adopting this library provides a stable, feature-rich experience across various form factors,…

entries.title: AEP guideline: Cast Support
entries.summary: Integrate Google Cast support into video and audio centric applications by leveraging the Media3 library. This elevates the app's media experience by offering a frictionless streaming experience, allowing users to effortlessly cast video and audio from mobile devices to larger screens like TVs and s…

entries.title: AEP guideline: Dark Theme Support
entries.summary: Implement a comprehensive **dark theme** that reduces luminance by shifting the UI to dark grays or low tonal values to improve legibility and reduce eye strain. Your app must automatically adopt the system-wide dark theme preference.

entries.title: AEP guideline: Edge to Edge
entries.summary: Implement edge-to-edge drawing by default to maximize screen space and achieve a modern, premium, bezel-less aesthetic. By extending the UI canvas behind the system navigation and status bars, apps provide a more immersive experience fundamental to a high-quality feel.

entries.title: AEP guideline: Engage SDK
entries.summary: Integrate Engage SDK to deliver personalized recommendations and continuation content directly to users across multiple on-device surfaces, like Collections, Entertainment Space, and the Play Store. entries.title: AEP guideline: Feature Availability
entries.summary: Maintain feature consistency on Android relative to comparable non-Android platforms to ensure a consistent and premium experience for users regardless of their device choice.

entries.title: AEP guideline: Form Factor Support
entries.summary: Implement high-quality experiences across multiple form factors to reduce fragmentation and meet user expectations for a seamless multidevice ecosystem. entries.title: AEP guideline: Quality - Jetpack Compose

entries.title: AEP guideline: Material UX
entries.summary: Use Material Design 3, Android's recommended design system, to build beautiful, usable, accessible, and consistent products.

entries.title: AEP guideline: Media3
entries.summary: Integrate the Jetpack Media3 library as the standard for media playback, sessions, editing, metadata, and frame extraction to ensure apps utilize recommended APIs for handling complex media tasks across Android form factors. entries.title: AEP guideline: Title Availability

entries.title: AEP guideline: Night Mode
entries.summary: Adopt Night Mode to enable hardware-accelerated low light photo capture with the in-app camera, ensuring your app leverages the same advanced image processing as the native camera app for high-quality results in low-light environments.

entries.title: AEP guideline: Phishing Resistant Authentication
entries.summary: In addition to any existing sign-in methods, implement a phishing-resistant authentication method, specifically FIDO or WebAuthn-based passkey, or Single Sign-On from an accepted federated identity provider such as Sign in with Google.

entries.title: AEP guideline: Photo Picker
entries.summary: Integrate the Android photo picker to provide users with a consistent, secure, and feature-rich way to select photos and videos. By adopting this standard interface, apps can allow users to access both local and cloud-backed assets while enhancing privacy through reduced permission requirements.

entries.title: AEP guideline: Physics Based Motion
entries.summary: Implement physics-based motion dynamics for primary user interactions to elevate the perceived quality, fluidity, and responsiveness of your apps. Use spring-driven mechanics instead of duration-based, fixed easing curves to ensure that motion preserves the momentum of the user's gesture and remains…

entries.title: AEP guideline: Picture in Picture Support (PiP)
entries.summary: Adopt Picture-in-Picture (PiP) mode to ensure that apps featuring primary video playback, video calling, communication, or active navigation allow content to seamlessly shrink to a floating window when a user navigates away. This functionality is critical for modern multi-tasking, preventing a restr…

entries.title: AEP guideline: Play Content
entries.summary: Implement Play Content discovery experiences to enhance visibility of your content on the Play Store. By integrating with Play Content, apps can showcase their digital offerings through various programs like Promo Content or Play Shorts, helping users discover relevant content more effectively.

entries.title: AEP guideline: Predictive Back
entries.summary: Adopt the predictive back-to-home gesture to provide a smoother, more predictable navigation experience. entries.title: AEP guideline: Preload Caching

entries.title: AEP guideline: Restore Credentials
entries.summary: Implement the Restore Credentials API to provide an automatic sign in experience for users during Android-to-Android transition or app restoration. entries.title: AEP guideline: Share Sheet
entries.summary: Use [Android Sharesheet](https://developer.android.com/training/sharing/send) for any general content-sharing flow to provide a consistent, faster, and more secure experience for users.

entries.title: AEP guideline: Stability Requirements

entries.title: Register for limited distribution on Android devices
entries.summary: We're committed to keeping Android an open platform for you to learn, experiment, and build for fun. If you are a developer who doesn't distribute apps widely, we recognize that your needs may be different from commercial developers and have provided an account type for you.

entries.title: Cloud save
entries.summary: Players play on multiple devices and install a game on same device multiple times. As a result, they always need to start their game from their last progress state. To achieve this, you must implement a cloud save solution in your game.
